frozen_components: Large Language Model (LLM) hidden states are read but not modified
architecture.summary: The paper systematically evaluates Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) applied to Large Language Models for interpretable feature extraction in classification tasks. SAEs decompose LLM hidden states into sparse, interpretable features. The framework extracts features from different model layers, applies various pooling strategies and binarization thresholds to convert continuous SAE activations into discrete signals, and tests these features as input to downstream classifiers. Features are evaluated on safety-critical classification (toxicity detection) and shown to transfer across model scales, languages, and modalities.
trained_components: Downstream classifier on extracted SAE features

biblio.title: Sparse Autoencoder Features for Classifications and Transferability

claims.statement: SAE-derived features achieve macro F1 > 0.8, outperforming hidden-state and BoW baselines
SAE-derived features demonstrate cross-model transfer from Gemma 2 2B to 9B-IT models
Features generalize in a zero-shot manner to cross-lingual toxicity detection tasks
Features generalize in a zero-shot manner to visual classification tasks
Pooling strategies have significant impact on performance
Binarization of continuous SAE activations offers an efficient alternative to traditional feature selection while maintaining or improving performance
The framework systematically analyzes model-layer selection and scaling properties for SAE-based feature extraction
